target.title: fix(agents): recover DeepSeek DSML tool markup into synthetic tool calls
target.body:
## Summary
`microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro` can emit DSML tool markup as plain assistant text on the `openai-completions` route instead of native `tool_calls`. This PR adds a conservative recovery path that turns complete, valid DSML `invoke` blocks into synthetic `toolCall` blocks while preserving surrounding visible text and emitting every valid invoke in the response.

This branch now also includes the missing Foundry fallback fix that stops the generic DeepSeek V4 thinking wrapper from injecting unsupported `payload.thinking` on the Foundry-hosted OpenAI-compatible route. Without that, the real CLI proof for the DSML fix was blocked earlier by `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking` before tool execution could even be exercised.

## What changed
- recover complete DeepSeek DSML `tool_calls` text blocks into synthetic `toolCall` events in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- preserve visible text around recovered DSML markup
- emit every valid recovered `invoke` entry in a DSML block
- keep malformed or incomplete DSML non-executable
- skip the generic DeepSeek V4 thinking wrapper on the `microsoft-foundry` fallback path so the real Foundry route can be exercised without the earlier request-shaping failure
- add focused regression tests for both the DSML recovery behavior and the Foundry fallback-path guard

## Real runtime proof
### Environment
- machine: local macOS operator environment
- runtime path exercised: local CLI via source checkout at `/Users/manos/Code/openclaw`
- patched build tested: `OpenClaw 2026.5.24 (5f09fa9)`
- exact commit tested: `5f09fa9935943402805d39ea2d1e280b5a45249d`
- provider/model exercised: `microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro`

### Before
Installed CLI on this machine before using the patched build:
- `openclaw --version` -> `OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (a374c3a)`

Same live tool-use command on the installed binary failed on the affected Foundry route with:
- `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking`

That showed the route was still blocked before tool execution on the older local runtime.

### After
Patched build command:
```bash
node /Users/manos/Code/openclaw/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking minimal --message 'Call the session_status tool exactly once with sessionKey current and then stop.' --json
```

Observed result on the patched build:
- request completed successfully on `microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro`
- `toolSummary.calls` = `1`
- `toolSummary.tools` = `["session_status"]`
- final assistant visible text: `Current model: \`microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro\`.`
- execution trace showed a successful single-attempt run on the requested provider/model with no fallback

This proves the real Foundry tool-use surface now gets past the earlier request-shaping failure and actually executes the requested tool on the patched build.

### Remaining proof gap
I did **not** capture the raw provider stream for the successful patched run, so I cannot honestly claim from this proof artifact alone whether Foundry emitted native `tool_calls` or DSML text that then flowed through the recovery path. What I can claim is narrower and true:
- this exact real CLI route previously failed to reach tool execution locally
- on the patched build it now successfully executes the requested tool and returns grounded output from that tool
- focused unit coverage still directly exercises the DSML-text recovery path itself, including multiple invokes and visible-text preservation

## Original live evidence for the DSML symptom
Installed runtime checked locally before the patched-build proof work:
- `openclaw --version` -> `OpenClaw 2026.5.20 (e510042)`

Tool-use repro against the real CLI/runtime:
```bash
openclaw agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking minimal --message 'Call the session_status tool exactly once with sessionKey current and then stop.' --json
```

Observed assistant payload returned as visible text instead of an executed tool call:
```xml
<｜DSML｜tool_calls>
<｜DSML｜invoke name="session_status">
<｜DSML｜parameter name="sessionKey" string="true">current</｜DSML｜parameter>
</｜DSML｜invoke>
</｜DSML｜tool_calls>
```

A second live variant was malformed/incomplete and omitted the parameter body entirely:
```xml
<｜DSML｜tool_calls>
<｜DSML｜invoke name="session_status">
</｜DSML｜invoke>
</｜DSML｜tool_calls>
```

Subagent/history verification also showed that the tool was not actually executed; the markup was emitted as assistant text.

## Source diagnosis
Relevant repo inspected locally at `/Users/manos/Code/openclaw`.

### What the current code did before this patch
`src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.ts` strips DSML wrapper text from visible output.

`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` used that filter in `processOpenAICompletionsStream(...)`, but executable tool calls still came only from native `choiceDelta.tool_calls`.

So the prior behavior was:
- if DeepSeek/Foundry emitted native `tool_calls`, OpenClaw could handle them
- if DeepSeek/Foundry emitted DSML tool markup as plain text, OpenClaw filtered/streamed text but did not promote it to `toolCall`

### Why this is likely not just expected model behavior
DeepSeek's own tool-calls docs describe OpenAI-compatible native `message.tool_calls` as the intended path:
- https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/tool_calls

There is also upstream evidence that DeepSeek V4 Pro can intermittently emit tool calls as plain text with `tool_calls: null`:
- https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3/issues/1244

So the remaining problem looks like a combination of:
1. upstream/provider instability in DeepSeek tool emission
2. missing defensive recovery in OpenClaw for DSML/textual tool intent on the `openai-completions` stream path

## Validation
Targeted tests run locally:
```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t 'DeepSeek DSML|tool calls'
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts -t 'DeepSeek V4|MiMo V2.6'
git diff --check
pnpm build
```

Results:
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`: 2 files passed, 18 tests passed, 374 skipped
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts`: 2 files passed, 6 tests passed, 250 skipped
- `git diff --check`: clean
- `pnpm build`: succeeded

## Labels / framing suggestion
- bug
- providers
- microsoft-foundry
- deepseek
- streaming
- tool-calls
target.author: silvesterxm
